question: Review this Dutch distribution agreement excerpt: 'Distributor shall indemnify Supplier against all claims, damages, and costs arising from Distributor's marketing activities, without any limitation. Governing law: Dutch law. Disputes: NAI Arbitration Amsterdam. Non-compete: Distributor shall not sell competing products for 5 years after termination in the entire EU.'

expectations.evidence: Section 1 is entirely dedicated to the unlimited indemnity clause, explicitly rated RED. Quotes 'without any limitation' multiple times. States 'blanket, unlimited indemnity' and references Art. 6:248 lid 2 BW. RED flag called out in Key Risks section: 'RED — Unlimited Indemnity'.

Section 3 rated RED: 'Five years post-termination is plainly excessive for a distribution agreement.' States '5-year EU-wide non-compete is unacceptable in the circumstances.' References disproportionate multiple times. States 'manifestly disproportionate' for EU-wide scope.

Extensive competition law analysis: Art. 6 Mededingingswet cited multiple times, Art. 101 TFEU, VBER 2022 (Commission Regulation (EU) 2022/720), ACM (Autoriteit Consument & Markt), cartel prohibition, de minimis thresholds, mededingingsrecht mentioned in escalation notice. Full subsection (c) dedicated to 'Competition law violation — Art. 6 Mw and Art. 101 TFEU'.
Full disclaimer at end: 'IMPORTANT — NOT LEGAL ADVICE. This analysis is generated by an AI system and is provided for general informational purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice...'

quality_notes: Outstanding output on all dimensions. Legal accuracy is excellent throughout: the VBER 2022 reference (Regulation (EU) 2022/720, in force June 2022) is correctly cited with the accurate 1-year post-termination safe harbor provision (Art. 5(1)(b) VBER 2022) — this is a nuanced, up-to-date legal point that demonstrates deep knowledge. The Art. 6 Mededingingswet / Art. 101 TFEU parallel cartel prohibition analysis is precise, including the nullity consequence under Art. 6 lid 2 Mw / Art. 101(2) TFEU. The distinction between void (nietig) vs. voidable (vernietigbaar) consequences is correctly applied. The Art. 7:443 BW agency non-compete 2-year maximum is correctly noted with the appropriate qualifier that it applies directly only to agentuurovereenkomsten but is applied by analogy to distribution agreements — an accurate and sophisticated observation. The Art. 6:248 lid 2 BW application as a fallback to competition law analysis shows comprehensive legal thinking. Completeness is very strong: covers all four clauses from the excerpt (indemnity, governing law, arbitration, non-compete), plus identifies 8 missing clauses including the important agentuurovereenkomst classification issue and klantenvergoeding risk. Actionability: 6 specific recommended actions with escalation to competition law counsel. Citation depth: Art. 6 Mw, Art. 101 TFEU, VBER 2022 Art. 5(1)(b), Art. 7:443 BW, Art. 7:428-445 BW, Art. 7:437 BW, Art. 7:445 BW, Art. 6:248 lid 2 BW, Art. 6:101 BW, Art. 6:74 BW, Art. 6:94 BW, Art. 6:96 BW, ECLI:NL:HR:2013:BY8101 (Lundiform/Mexx), 1020-1076 Rv. Cross-referencing: references competition law, agency law, arbitration law, product liability, and correctly identifies the need for mededingingsrecht specialist counsel. An escalation notice is appropriately included for the competition law violation risk.
